body:
In **Alameda County, California** , garage door spring replacement typically falls in a fairly consistent range across the Bay Area, since pricing is driven more by labor and spring type than the exact city.

### Typical cost range (Alameda County)

  * **$150 – $350** → most common range for a standard single garage door spring job [How Much Does It Cost to Replace a Garage Door Spring? (2025 Data)](https://www.homeadvisor.com/cost/garages/garage-door-spring-repair/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

  * **$200 – $500** → torsion spring replacement (more common in newer homes / heavier doors) [Garage Door Spring Replacement Cost (2026) - Complete Price... | TheCostGuide](https://thecostguide.com/home/windows-doors/garage-door-spring-replacement-cost/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

  * **$120 – $200** → extension spring replacement (usually cheaper, older-style systems) [How Much Does Garage Door Spring Replacement Cost? (2026)](https://homeguide.com/costs/garage-door-spring-replacement-cost?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

### What most homeowners actually pay locally

In the Bay Area (including Oakland/Alameda County), real-world quotes often cluster around:

  * **~$200 – $330 for a single door with standard springs** [Garage Door Spring Replacement Cost in Oakland, California](https://www.homeblue.com/garage-door-spring/oakland-ca-garage-door-spring-replacement-cost.htm?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

  * **~$250 – $450 for a heavier or double-car door**

### What changes the price

Your final cost depends mostly on:

  * **Spring type** (torsion costs more but lasts longer)

  * **Door size** (double doors = higher tension/heavier springs)

  * **How many springs break** (usually both are replaced together)

  * **Emergency/same-day service fees**

  * Optional add-ons (cables, rollers, tune-up)

### Quick takeaway

For Alameda County specifically, a realistic expectation is:

> **About $200–$400 total for most standard residential spring replacements**

If you want, tell me your garage door type (single/double, torsion vs extension if you know it), and I can narrow it to a tighter estimate.
